lines.english: Those who describe You, remain absorbed in You. ||1||
No one can measure Your Worth, or describe You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡੇ ਮੇਰੇ ਸਾਹਿਬਾ ਗਹਿਰ ਗੰਭੀਰਾ ਗੁਣੀ ਗਹੀਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: vadde mere saahibaa gahir ganbheeraa gunee gaheeraa |
lines.english: O my Great Lord and Master of Unfathomable Depth, You are the Ocean of Excellence.
All the contemplators met together and practiced contemplation;

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਈ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਤੇਰਾ ਕੇਤਾ ਕੇਵਡੁ ਚੀਰਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: koee na jaanai teraa ketaa kevadd cheeraa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: No one knows the greatness of Your expanse. ||1||Pause||
all the appraisers met together and tried to appraise You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਣੁ ਨ ਜਾਈ ਤੇਰੀ ਤਿਲੁ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: kehan na jaaee teree til vaddiaaee |2|
lines.english: could not express even an iota of Your Greatness. ||2||
The theologians, the meditators and the teachers of teachers

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਿ ਸਤ ਸਭਿ ਤਪ ਸਭਿ ਚੰਗਿਆਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: sabh sat sabh tap sabh changiaaeea |
lines.english: All Truth, all austerities, all goodness,
and the greatness of the Siddhas, the beings of perfect spiritual powers

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਧੁ ਵਿਣੁ ਸਿਧੀ ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਪਾਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: tudh vin sidhee kinai na paaeea |
lines.english: without You, none has attained such spiritual powers.
What can the helpless speaker do?
Your bounties are overflowing with Your Praises.
They are obtained by Your Grace; their flow cannot be blocked. ||3||
O Nanak, the True Lord is the Embellisher. ||4||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਤੂੰ ਦੇਹਿ ਤਿਸੈ ਕਿਆ ਚਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: jis toon dehi tisai kiaa chaaraa |
lines.english: And the one, unto whom You give - why should he think of any other?
Aasaa, First Mehl:
Chanting the Name, I live; forgetting it, I die.
It is so difficult to chant the True Name.
If someone feels hunger for the True Name,
then that hunger shall consume his pains. ||1||
People have grown weary of trying to appraise the greatness of the True Name,
True is the Master, and True is His Name. ||1||Pause||
So how could I ever forget Him, O my Mother?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਸਭਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਕੈ ਆਖਣ ਪਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: je sabh mil kai aakhan paeh |
lines.english: Even if they were all to meet together and recount them,

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡਾ ਨ ਹੋਵੈ ਘਾਟਿ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: vaddaa na hovai ghaatt na jaae |2|
lines.english: You would not be made any greater or lesser. ||2||
But they have not been able to appraise even an iota of it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਕੋ ਹੋਆ ਨਾ ਕੋ ਹੋਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: naa ko hoaa naa ko hoe |3|
lines.english: there has never been anyone like Him, and there never shall be. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਓਹੁ ਮਰੈ ਨ ਹੋਵੈ ਸੋਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: naa ohu marai na hovai sog |
lines.english: He does not die - there is no reason to mourn.
This Glorious Virtue is His alone - no one else is like Him;
He continues to give, but His Provisions are never exhausted.
As Great as You Yourself are, so Great are Your Gifts.
Those who forget their Lord and Master are vile and despicable.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਦਿਨੁ ਕਰਿ ਕੈ ਕੀਤੀ ਰਾਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: jin din kar kai keetee raat |
lines.english: It is You who created day and night as well.
O Nanak, without the Name, people are wretched outcasts. ||4||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਦਰਿ ਮਾਂਗਤੁ ਕੂਕ ਕਰੇ ਮਹਲੀ ਖਸਮੁ ਸੁਣੇ ॥
lines.translit: je dar maangat kook kare mahalee khasam sune |
lines.english: If a beggar cries out at the door, the Master hears it in His Mansion.
Aasaa, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਵੈ ਧੀਰਕ ਭਾਵੈ ਧਕੇ ਏਕ ਵਡਾਈ ਦੇਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: bhaavai dheerak bhaavai dhake ek vaddaaee dee |1|
lines.english: Whether He receives him or pushes him away, it is the Gift of the Lord's Greatness. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਣਹੁ ਜੋਤਿ ਨ ਪੂਛਹੁ ਜਾਤੀ ਆਗੈ ਜਾਤਿ ਨ ਹੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jaanahu jot na poochhahu jaatee aagai jaat na he |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Recognize the Lord's Light within all, and do not consider social class or status; there are no classes or castes in the world hereafter. ||1||Pause||
He Himself considers our complaints.
He Himself acts, and He Himself inspires us to act.
Since You, O Creator Lord, are the Doer,
why should I submit to the world? ||2||
You Yourself created and You Yourself give.
You Yourself eliminate evil-mindedness;
by Guru's Grace, You come to abide in our minds,
Unto others, the Truth is not bestowed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਕਿਸੈ ਦੇਇ ਵਖਾਣੈ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਆਗੈ ਪੂਛ ਨ ਲੇਇ ॥੪॥੩॥
lines.translit: je kisai dee vakhaanai naanak aagai poochh na lee |4|3|
lines.english: If He bestows it upon someone, says Nanak, then, in the world hereafter, that person is not called to account. ||4||3||
He Himself infuses love for the Truth.
and then, pain and darkness are dispelled from within. ||3||
the drum of the world resounds with the beat.
The urges of the heart are like cymbals and ankle-bells;
Aasaa, First Mehl:
where can the celibates and the men of truth place their feet? ||1||
Naarad dances to the tune of the Dark Age of Kali Yuga;
Nanak is a sacrifice to the Naam, the Name of the Lord.
out of love for bread, he comes to dwell in his home.
The disciple feeds on the Guru;
The world is blind; our Lord and Master is All-seeing. ||1||Pause||
